hook_template: Dental practice angle: independent dentists are losing new patients to Bupa, MyDentist, Smile Direct because their websites look ten years older. Hook line: a punchy stat or claim about losing patients. Two short body lines. Tie back to mobile-first design.

data.caption: of new patients judge a dental practice on the website before they book.

hook_template: Dental angle: patients want to book online in under 30 seconds. Most dental sites require calling during 9-5 hours when patients are at work. Friction kills bookings. Punchy hook, then 2-3 short observations.

data.quote: If booking takes more than thirty seconds, they're already gone.

hook_template: Dental angle: dental searches happen on mobile, in pain, late at night. Your site must load fast and put the phone number above the fold. Hook line then quick observations.

Med spa angle: clients are spending £200-£2000 per treatment. Trust is everything. List 2-3 trust signals most med spa sites miss: real before/afters, named practitioners with credentials, GMC numbers, real prices.
Med spa angle: if booking a consultation takes more than 30 seconds, they are gone. Specific. Snap.

data.quote: Booking in thirty seconds. Or they're gone.

hook_template: Med spa angle: hiding prices behind 'enquire for pricing' lost you the booking three clicks ago. The clinic that shows £75 from line one wins. Punchy.
Physio angle: ranking for 'sports massage Reading' or '[your town] sports physio' beats ranking for 'physiotherapy'. Local intent converts. Hook then one tactical line.
Physio angle: one service per page beats a single 'services' menu. Each page can rank for its own keyword. Sports massage. Post-op rehab. Bike fitting. Punchy.

data.quote: One service. One page. One keyword.

hook_template: Physio angle: searches like 'ACL recovery Reading' or 'sports massage Wokingham' beat ranking for the generic word 'physiotherapy'. Niche the page, win the search. Punch the hook.

data.quote: 'ACL recovery Reading' beats 'physiotherapy'. Every time.

hook_template: Physio angle: patients want named therapists with credentials. Not 'meet the team' photos with first names only. Show full names, HCPC registration, what each clinician actually treats.
Personal trainer angle: your Instagram is doing the heavy lifting. Your bio sends people to a dead landing page that does not convert. Snap line then one observation.
PT angle: show your prices. Stop the 'message for prices' nonsense. Clients self-qualify when prices are visible. You waste fewer DMs.

data.quote: Show your prices. Stop the 'message for prices' nonsense.

hook_template: PT angle: your Instagram bio link sends people to a slow homepage. They bounce in under 2 seconds. Six months of audience building, lost at the door.
PT angle: niche personal trainers — postnatal, athletes, over-50s, hybrid lifters — book out faster than generalists. The site needs to lead with the niche, not 'one-to-one personal training'.

data.caption: Niche PTs book out three times faster than generalists.

hook_template: Builder angle: clients want to see your work. Not read about your values. Big photos, brief project descriptions, postcode of the job, that is the site you need.
Builder angle: three reasons builders do not get quote requests through their site. 1: no photos of recent work. 2: no clear contact form. 3: site loads in 6 seconds. Tight list.
Builder angle: one page per service area beats one generic 'we cover the Thames Valley' line. 'Loft conversions Reading'. 'Extensions Wokingham'. Each one ranks for its postcode.

data.quote: One page per area. 'Loft conversions Reading'. Done.

hook_template: Builder angle: embed Google reviews on the homepage. Stop sending visitors away to your Google Business Profile to read them. The visit that leaves to read reviews rarely comes back.
Nail salon angle: your website looks like a 2014 Wix template. Pink gradient. Stock photo of a manicure. Comic Sans tagline. Snap commentary then a brand observation.
Nail salon angle: taking all bookings through Instagram DMs leaks margin to Meta and wastes your time. Your own site with a booking calendar is twenty quid a month, not lost commission.

data.quote: DM bookings cost you margin. Every single time.

hook_template: Nail salon angle: your nail work IS the portfolio. Big visual grid on the homepage. Not a 'gallery' menu link buried two clicks deep. Show the work above the fold.

data.quote: Your nail work is the portfolio. Show it.

hook_template: Nail salon angle: 'Are you open for walk-ins right now?' is the question every salon site needs to answer. Most don't. Live availability beats every 'about us' page ever written.
Barber angle: your site only needs to answer one question. 'Are you open right now?' Address, hours, walk-in or appointment. That is the whole site.
Barber angle: every barber site in the UK uses the same template. Same brick wall photo. Same vintage chair photo. Why blend in. Snap line then observation.

data.quote: Every barber site looks the same. Yours doesn't have to.

hook_template: Barber angle: nobody books a haircut from a desktop. Design entirely for the phone. Ignore the desktop demo. Force every decision through the mobile screen first.

data.caption: of haircut bookings happen on a phone. Design for it.

hook_template: Barber angle: your price board hangs above the chair. It belongs on the homepage too. Same prices, same order. Consistency builds trust before the first visit.

data.quote: Your price board belongs on the homepage too.

hook_template: Beautician angle: 12 services on the homepage is too many. Pick your top three. Lead with them. The rest live on inner pages. Cognitive load kills bookings.

data.quote: Twelve services on the homepage is too many.

hook_template: Beautician angle: your beauty business deserves a site that does not look like a Squarespace template. Editorial. Mobile-first. Built around your service photography.
Beautician angle: treatment result photos belong on the service page. Not three clicks deep in a 'gallery'. Show the result next to the treatment description, every time.

data.quote: Result photos on the service page. Not three clicks deep.

hook_template: Beautician angle: package pricing converts better than per-treatment pricing. Bundle three sessions. Show the saving. Anchor the per-treatment price against the package.
Cleaning services angle: this is a trust game. Photos of the actual team, postcode coverage, real reviews with names, transparent pricing. Sites without these get ignored.
Cleaners angle: 'request a quote' loses to 'from £18 an hour'. Show pricing. Convert browsers into bookers.
Cleaners angle: list every postcode you cover, as a real page each. 'Office cleaning RG1'. 'Domestic cleaning RG2'. Each one ranks. Local intent converts.
Cleaners angle: replace 'request a quote' with a 30-second calculator. Bedrooms, frequency, postcode. Show the estimate immediately. Triple the inbound lead rate.
Accountants angle: your site reads like a 1995 law firm. 'Bespoke solutions for the modern enterprise'. Clients want plain English. Drop the corporate voice.
Accountant angle: niche down your accounting site. 'Accountant for dentists in the South East' beats 'accountant'. Specificity ranks and converts.

data.quote: Niche down. Generalist accountants get ignored.

hook_template: Accountant angle: tax deadlines drive search. Self-assessment 31 January. VAT quarterly. Payroll weekly. A deadline page for each. Rank for the question, win the client.

data.quote: Rank for the deadline. Win the client.

hook_template: Accountant angle: visible package pricing. Sole trader £60 a month. Limited company £120 a month. Hidden pricing tells prospects 'too expensive'. Show it.

data.caption: Sole trader package. Limited company £120/mo. Visible pricing.

hook_template: Electrician angle: the emergency call comes from a mobile, at 11pm, from someone with no power. Your site must load in under 2 seconds. Phone number must be tappable above the fold.
Electrician angle: Gas Safe, NICEIC, Part P. Real credentials in the header. Not hidden in a footer 'about us' page. Trust above the fold or you lose the call.

data.quote: NICEIC. Part P. Trustmark. In the header. Not the footer.

hook_template: Electrician angle: one page per town. 'Electrician Reading'. 'Electrician Wokingham'. 'EV charger installer Maidenhead'. Local intent ranks fast. Generic catchment pages do not.

data.quote: Electrician Reading. Electrician Wokingham. One page each.

hook_template: Electrician angle: photos of finished installations. Consumer units. EV chargers. Smart home wiring. Visual proof beats credentials alone. Hang the work on the homepage.
Pain point: every 1 second of load time costs 7 percent of conversions. Reference Akamai. Punch the stat. Tie to the service business owner.

data.caption: of conversions lost per second of load time. (Akamai)

hook_template: Pain point: Wix and Squarespace templates ship 6MB of bloat your visitors do not need. Custom builds load in a fraction of the weight. Specific, technical, confident.
Pain point: 80 percent of UK service business traffic is mobile. Most agency sites still get designed on a 27 inch monitor. The result is obvious on a phone.

data.caption: of UK service business traffic is mobile.

hook_template: Pain point: bounce rate over 70 percent means your homepage does not answer the visitor's question in the first 2 seconds. Direct line. Then one fix.

data.caption: bounce rate means your homepage doesn't answer the question.

hook_template: Pain point: GDPR cookie banners that nuke your mobile conversion rate. Half the screen is a banner. Visitors leave before they ever see the hero.

data.quote: Your cookie banner is eating half the screen.

hook_template: Demo showcase: Thorne Dental. 100/100 Lighthouse mobile. Live demo. One short line about what makes it score. Punchy.
Demo showcase: Thorne Dental's booking flow. Three taps from homepage to appointment confirmed. No phone call, no opening hours dance.

data.quote: Thorne Dental: three taps to a booked appointment.

hook_template: Demo showcase: Linden Aesthetics. Med spa site that respects the buyer. Pricing visible. Practitioner credentials in the header. Real photography area.
Demo showcase: how Linden Aesthetics builds trust without a single stock photo. Named practitioners. Real GMC numbers. Editorial confidence.
Demo showcase: Kestrel Physio. One service per page. Each one ranking locally. Local SEO done by structure, not by stuffing keywords.
Demo showcase: Kestrel Physio. Sports massage. Post-op rehab. Bike fitting. Each one a standalone page. Each one ranks for its own keyword in its town.

data.quote: Sports massage. Post-op rehab. Bike fitting. Each its own page.

hook_template: Demo showcase: Forge PT. Built for trainers whose audience comes from Instagram. Loud. Bold. Mobile first. Books in three taps.
Demo showcase: Forge PT puts prices on the homepage. £60 a session. £500 a month. Visitors qualify themselves before they message.

data.caption: per session. £500 per month. On the homepage. Forge PT.

hook_template: Demo showcase: Marne & Lyle. Editorial layout for a beauty brand. Big type. Quiet whitespace. Confident.
Demo showcase: Marne & Lyle. Built like a print magazine. Reads like one too. Why service brands deserve editorial design.

data.quote: Built like a print magazine. Reads like one.

hook_template: Case study: Amber Oak. Before our retrofit: 4.2 second load time, 38 percent bounce on mobile, 4 quote requests a month. Old WordPress build. Big hero video. Cookie banner that ate half the screen.

data.caption: Amber Oak before. 38% bounce. Four quotes a month.

hook_template: Case study: Amber Oak. After our retrofit: 0.9 second load, 14 percent bounce, 23 quote requests a month. Same content. New build. 60 days.
Case study: Ridgeline. Before: looked great on the desktop demo. Ranked nowhere. Bounced everyone on mobile. The site existed to be admired, not to convert.

data.quote: Looked great on desktop. Ranked nowhere. Bounced on mobile.

hook_template: Case study: Ridgeline. After: top 3 organic for every service in their town. 6x more quote requests. We rebuilt mobile-first and let desktop inherit. The right order.
Flex: all five Sitewisers demos hit 100/100 Lighthouse on mobile. Most agencies who sell 'fast websites' score 60. Open the inspector. Check.

data.subtitle: Five demos. Every score. Every device.

hook_template: Flex: Lighthouse 100 is not vanity. It is a proxy for everything that matters. Load time. Layout shift. Accessibility. Tap target sizes. Site speed wins bookings.

data.subtitle: Lighthouse 100 is not vanity. It is the floor.

hook_template: Flex: Lighthouse 100 mobile vs Lighthouse 60. The conversion gap is enormous. Specific numbers. Punch.
Flex: any agency can claim fast websites. Open Chrome DevTools, run Lighthouse on their portfolio. The score is the only honest answer. Direct call-out.

data.quote: Open DevTools. Run Lighthouse. The score is the answer.

hook_template: Stat: 1 second of load time costs 11 percent of pageviews. Akamai data. Tight commentary, tie to service businesses.

data.caption: of pageviews lost per second of load time. (Akamai)

hook_template: Stat: 53 percent of mobile visitors abandon a site that takes over 3 seconds to load. Google data. Tie to UK service business behaviour.

data.caption: of mobile users leave a site that loads over 3 seconds. (Google)

hook_template: Stat: 75 percent of users judge a business's credibility based on website design alone. Stanford research. Punch the implication.

data.caption: of users judge your credibility on design alone. (Stanford)

hook_template: Stat: UK service business sites average a 58 percent bounce rate. Sitewisers demo sites average 24. The difference is the design.
Quick tip: one primary CTA per page. Not four. Choice paralysis kills conversions. Punch hook then one line of why.

data.quote: One CTA per page. Stop offering four choices.

hook_template: Quick tip: if your hero is a rotating slideshow, kill it. Nobody waits for slide 3. Pick your strongest message, lock it.

data.quote: If your hero is a slideshow, kill it. Nobody waits for slide 3.

hook_template: Quick tip: contact forms with more than 5 fields cut conversions in half. Cut the dropdown. Cut the company size question. Cut anything you do not act on.

data.caption: fewer conversions when your form has more than five fields.

hook_template: Quick tip: your phone number must be tappable on every single page. Mobile users do not retype 11 digits. Sticky header with a phone link.

data.quote: Your phone number must be tappable on every page.

hook_template: Quick tip: compress your images. Most service business sites ship 8MB hero images that could be 200KB without visible loss. WebP, AVIF, sized for the viewport.
Quick tip: if your prices are not on your site, your prospect's first impression is 'too expensive'. Even a 'from £X' line beats nothing.

data.quote: No prices on your site? First impression is 'too expensive'.

hook_template: Observation: every UK web design agency site looks the same. Big hero video. 'We craft digital experiences'. A team photo. Snap commentary.

data.quote: Every UK agency site looks the same. Big hero video. Team photo. Boring.

hook_template: Observation: stop chasing design trends. Parallax. Cursor effects. Locomotive scroll. Service businesses need clarity. Trends slow sites down.

data.quote: Stop chasing trends. Service businesses need clarity.

hook_template: Observation: starter site, £1,200. Most agencies quote £5k for the same scope, deliver slower, and use the same Webflow templates we built ours from. Direct.

data.caption: Starter site. Same scope. Most agencies quote £5k.

hook_template: Observation: Growth package, £2,000 plus £400 a month. Includes Lighthouse 100 guarantee. If we drop a score, we fix it. No retainer required without value.
Observation: 'mobile-first' is the most-claimed-and-least-practiced phrase in web design. If you opened the Figma on a desktop screen, it was not mobile-first.

data.quote: Mobile-first is the most-claimed and least-practiced phrase in design.

hook_template: Observation: stock photos are a tell. Generic smiling team. Generic handshake. Generic laptop. Visitors clock it instantly and trust evaporates. Real photos or none.

data.quote: Stock photos are a tell. Visitors clock it instantly.
